content:
Complete  the pending task under developing  i not want rork ia on my business  right now and Complete  this and deploy  live now send proof and verified # IVX IA SENIOR DEVELOPER — FINAL AI PROVIDER RECOVERY, DEPLOYMENT AND 100% VERIFICATION

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0
GOAL: Restore a working production AI provider for IVX Owner AI, remove broken fallback loops, deploy the fix, and prove IVX IA Senior Developer works end to end from the real IVX application.

CURRENT CONFIRMED FAILURES:

1. Current Vercel AI Gateway credential returns HTTP 401.
2. Rork toolkit proxy returns HTTP 402 insufficient balance.
3. Owner AI repeatedly loops through failed providers.
4. IVX Owner AI has no reliable production AI provider.
5. The application is not 100% verified while the AI provider is unavailable.

DO NOT:
- keep retrying the same expired credential
- route production permanently through Rork AI Cloud without owner approval
- expose any credential value
- put server credentials in Expo public variables
- mark VERIFIED while any provider returns 401 or 402
- return another plan without execution

==================================================
PHASE 1 — CAPTURE THE EXACT ACTIVE CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Record safely:

- active AI provider
- provider adapter
- model
- base URL
- credential variable name
- key prefix only
- deployment SHA
- Render deployment ID
- AI SDK version
- provider package version
- exact HTTP response
- exact provider error code
- trace ID

Never show the full key.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — TEST EVERY EXISTING PROVIDER CREDENTIAL
==================================================

Audit protected runtime variables for:

- VERCEL_AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
- AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
- OPENAI_API_KEY
- OPENAI_BASE_URL
- AI_PROVIDER
- AI_MODEL
- any approved alternate provider already stored

For each credential:

- present true/false
- non-empty true/false
- loaded at runtime true/false
- provider family
- compatible base URL
- compatible model
- real minimal API test
- HTTP status
- exact safe error
- PASS/FAIL

Do not ask the owner for a new credential unless every existing protected credential is tested and a real runtime result proves missing, expired, revoked, 401 or 403.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — CHOOSE ONE CANONICAL PRODUCTION PROVIDER
==================================================

Use exactly one validated provider configuration.

Option A — Vercel AI Gateway:
- valid Vercel gateway credential
- correct gateway base URL
- compatible provider adapter
- supported model identifier

Option B — Direct OpenAI:
- valid OpenAI API credential
- official OpenAI API base URL
- compatible OpenAI adapter
- supported model identifier

Do not mix:

- Vercel key with OpenAI direct endpoint
- OpenAI key with Vercel gateway endpoint
- Rork proxy credentials with either provider
- incompatible AI SDK/provider versions

The selected provider must pass a real minimal API request before deployment.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — REMOVE BROKEN RETRY LOOP
==================================================

Fix the provider-selection logic.

Required behavior:

1. Validate provider once at startup.
2. Mark invalid provider as unavailable after a confirmed 401/403.
3. Do not retry the same expired key repeatedly.
4. Use only explicitly configured approved fallback providers.
5. Maximum one controlled fallback attempt.
6. Return one clear failure with trace ID if all approved providers fail.
7. Never create an endless provider-selection loop.
8. Never display internal credential details to users.

Required states:

- PROVIDER_VALIDATING
- PROVIDER_READY
- PROVIDER_FAILED
- FALLBACK_VALIDATING
- FALLBACK_READY
- AI_UNAVAILABLE

==================================================
PHASE 5 — OWNER CONTROL FOR PROVIDER STATUS
==================================================

Add or verify an owner-only diagnostics section showing:

- provider
- model
- adapter version
- credential loaded true/false
- credential valid true/false
- last validation time
- last HTTP status
- fallback enabled true/false
- fallback used true/false
- trace ID

Do not reveal secret values.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — RUN PRODUCTION AI TESTS
==================================================

After selecting a valid provider, test:

1. Short text message
2. Long text message
3. Senior-developer task classification
4. Tool execution request
5. Image attachment analysis
6. PDF attachment analysis
7. Conversation persistence
8. Provider timeout behavior
9. Invalid-model behavior
10. Worker execution response

Required proof:

- request message ID
- response ID
- provider
- model
- HTTP status
- latency
- trace ID
- persisted true/false
- fallbackUsed true/false

==================================================
PHASE 7 — VERIFY SENIOR-DEVELOPER EXECUTION
==================================================

From the real production IVX Owner AI chat, execute one controlled safe task:

Task:
Add or update one harmless owner-only diagnostic field:

"ivxSeniorDeveloperProviderVerification": {
  "providerReady": true,
  "provider": "<active provider>",
  "model": "<active model>",
  "toolRegistryReady": true,
  "variablesValidated": true,
  "commitSha": "<runtime SHA>",
  "checkedAt": "<timestamp>"
}

The production IVX Owner AI must:

1. Authenticate owner.
2. Classify as developer task.
3. Start Live Work.
4. Inspect repository.
5. Change code.
6. Add test.
7. Run lint.
8. Run typecheck.
9. Run unit test.
10. Commit.
11. Push.
12. Deploy.
13. Verify live diagnostics.
14. Return structured proof.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — BUILD AND DEPLOY
==================================================

Run:

- dependency verification
- lint
- typecheck
- provider tests
- owner AI tests
- execution-worker tests
- integration tests
- backend smoke test
- production build

Then:

1. Commit approved fixes.
2. Push to owner-controlled GitHub.
3. Confirm remote SHA.
4. Trigger one Render deployment.
5. Prevent duplicate deployment for the same SHA.
6. Wait for completion.
7. Confirm deployed SHA equals GitHub SHA.
8. Verify /health.
9. Verify /readiness.
10. Verify Owner AI live message.
11. Verify controlled senior-developer task.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — RORK CLOUD DECISION
==================================================

Rork AI Cloud must not become the permanent production dependency unless the owner explicitly approves it.

If used temporarily:

- label it TEMPORARY
- record cost
- record provider limits
- record expiration/credit status
- preserve migration path back to the owner-controlled provider
- do not mark the architecture independent

Preferred final state:

- owner-controlled provider credential
- owner-controlled Render backend
- no Rork proxy dependency
- no Rork credit dependency

==================================================
PHASE 10 — DATA SAFETY
==================================================

Before and after deployment verify:

- members count
- investors count
- buyers count
- deals count
- messages count
- project videos count

Expected business-data changes:

ZERO

Do not alter authentication accounts, CRM data, investments, payments or deal records.

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL REPORT
==================================================

TASK UNDERSTOOD:
[exact provider recovery and execution scope]

ORIGINAL FAILURE:
- Provider:
- Model:
- Base URL:
- HTTP status:
- Error:
- Trace ID:

CREDENTIAL AUDIT:
1. Variable:
   Provider family:
   Present:
   Runtime loaded:
   Test status:
   HTTP status:
   Result:
2.

CANONICAL PROVIDER:
- Provider:
- Model:
- Base URL family:
- Adapter:
- Credential variable:
- Validation status:
- Fallback:
- Rork dependency: YES/NO

REMAINING ISSUES:

FINAL STATUS:
VERIFIED / FAILED / BLOCKED

100% VERIFIED RULE:

Use VERIFIED only when:

- one valid AI provider returns HTTP 200
- no active provider returns 401 or 402
- broken retry loop is removed
- Owner AI sends and receives a real production message
- attachments work
- persistence works
- production IVX Owner AI executes a controlled developer task
- commands return exit codes
- GitHub commit and remote SHA match
- Render deployment ID exists
- deployed SHA matches GitHub
- live diagnostics pass
- no Rork Cloud dependency remains unless owner-approved
- business data remains unchanged
- zero P0 or P1 issue remains
